title: Sri Vinayaka Temple, Guddattu
tagline: Sri Vinayaka Temple, Guddattu — spiritual town of Karnataka

overview.short: Guddattu Sri Vinayaka Temple is located at Kundapura, Udupi District in the state of Karnataka, India.
overview.description: Guddattu Sri Vinayaka Temple is located at Kundapura, Udupi District in the state of Karnataka, India. It is a Hindu temple dedicated to god Ganapathy, also called Jaladhivasa GanapathyTemple, It is the only Jaladhivasa Ganapathi temple in India. Lord Ganesha’s three-foot idol is believed to have emerged from the rock. Aayira Koda Seva, Tailabhyanjana, Panchamrutha and Rudrabhisheka are performed in the temple every day.

topPlaces.name: Kundapur
topPlaces.description: Kundapra, officially Kundapura, is a town in the Udupi district of Karnataka state in India. This town was known as Coondapoor when it was part of the erstwhile South Canara district (1862–1947) of the Madras Presidency of British India.

topPlaces.name: Vyasaraja Math (Kundapura)
topPlaces.description: Sri Vyasaraja Math (Kundapura) also known as Abbur Matha, (also written as Vyasaraja Matha (Kundapura) or Vyasaraja Mutt (Kundapura)) (Kannada: ಕುಂದಾಪುರ ವ್ಯಾಸರಾಜ ಮಠ) is one of the Dvaita Vedanta monasteries (matha) descended from Jagadguru Śrī Madhvācārya through Jayatirtha and Rajendra Tirtha (a disciple of Vidyadhiraja Tirtha) and further through Sridhara Tirtha and their disciples. Vyasaraja Matha got split into Vyasaraja Math (Sosale) and Vyasaraja Math (Kundapura) after Rama Tirtha.

topPlaces.name: Kemmannu
topPlaces.description: Kemmannu-Hoode-Bengre is a small village in the Udupi District of Karnataka State. It lies on the western coast of Karnataka, along the Suvarna River.

topPlaces.name: Panchagangavalli River
topPlaces.description: Panchagangavalli River is a river flowing through Kundapur and Gangolli in western India. The five rivers namely Souparnika River, Varahi River, Kedaka River, Chakra River and Kubja River join and merge into Arabian Sea.

topPlaces.name: Uppinakudru
topPlaces.description: Uppinakudru, is a small island located six kilometres north of Kundapura town in Udupi District of Karnataka.

topPlaces.name: Gangolli
topPlaces.description: Gangolli (also Ganguli) is a town in Kundapur Taluk of Udupi district in Karnataka state. It is situated at the estuary of the Panchagangavalli River.

topPlaces.name: Beejadi
topPlaces.description: Beejadi is a village in the southern state of Karnataka, India. It is located in the Kundapura taluk of Udupi district in Karnataka.

topPlaces.name: Basrur
topPlaces.description: Basrur / Basroor is a village in Kundapura taluk in Udupi district of Karnataka. Historically Basrur was also called Barcelor, Barcelore, Barcalor, Barzeloor, Basnur, Bares, Abu-Sarur and Barsellor.

items.note: Kundapra, officially Kundapura, is a town in the Udupi district of Karnataka state in India. This town was known as Coondapoor when it was part of the erstwhile South Canara district (1862–1947) of the Madras Presidency of British India.

Sri Vyasaraja Math (Kundapura) also known as Abbur Matha, (also written as Vyasaraja Matha (Kundapura) or Vyasaraja Mutt (Kundapura)) (Kannada: ಕುಂದಾಪುರ ವ್ಯಾಸರಾಜ ಮಠ) is one of the Dvaita Vedanta monasteries (matha) descended from Jagadguru Śrī Madhvācārya through Jayatirtha and Rajendra Tirtha (a disciple of Vidyadhiraja Tirtha) and further through Sridhara Tirtha and their disciples. Vyasaraja Matha got split into Vyasaraja Math (Sosale) and Vyasaraja Math (Kundapura) after Rama Tirtha.
Kemmannu-Hoode-Bengre is a small village in the Udupi District of Karnataka State. It lies on the western coast of Karnataka, along the Suvarna River.

Panchagangavalli River is a river flowing through Kundapur and Gangolli in western India. The five rivers namely Souparnika River, Varahi River, Kedaka River, Chakra River and Kubja River join and merge into Arabian Sea.
Uppinakudru, is a small island located six kilometres north of Kundapura town in Udupi District of Karnataka.
Gangolli (also Ganguli) is a town in Kundapur Taluk of Udupi district in Karnataka state. It is situated at the estuary of the Panchagangavalli River.
Beejadi is a village in the southern state of Karnataka, India. It is located in the Kundapura taluk of Udupi district in Karnataka.
Basrur / Basroor is a village in Kundapura taluk in Udupi district of Karnataka. Historically Basrur was also called Barcelor, Barcelore, Barcalor, Barzeloor, Basnur, Bares, Abu-Sarur and Barsellor.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Sri Vinayaka Temple, Guddattu?
faq.a: The best time to visit Sri Vinayaka Temple, Guddattu is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Sri Vinayaka Temple, Guddattu?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Mangaluru International Airport (77 km) and the nearest railway station is Mangaluru Central (86 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Sri Vinayaka Temple, Guddattu?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Sri Vinayaka Temple, Guddattu at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Sri Vinayaka Temple, Guddattu?
faq.a: Stays in Sri Vinayaka Temple, Guddattu start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Sri Vinayaka Temple, Guddattu?
faq.a: The top attractions are Kundapur, Vyasaraja Math (Kundapura), Kemmannu, Panchagangavalli River, Uppinakudru.
